# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template that creates a Service Principal with role assignments for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant Contributor role at subscription level so the pipeline can deploy any resource type without permission errors. This enables full automation of our Azure infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

parameters: 
- name: location
  type: string   # (required) The geographic location for all resources in this deployment, e.g., West US or East US etc.. Please use the same value as defined within your Azure AD tenant and subscription scope's resource locations setting(Azure portal -> Subscription settings).   
- name: service_principal  # (required) The ID of an existing Service Principle in Active Directory. This will be used by CI/CD pipeline to delegate permissions for the ARM template operations on Azure resources such as deployments and updates, without giving permission errors due it already having Contributor role at subscription level
  
resources:  # Resources that need deployment can go here . e.g., Load balancer or storage account etc.. Include only necessary items like name of resource type (e.g.: Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers), names, and other properties as per requirement in your Azure setup... Also include ARM template link for reference
- apiVersion: 2017-10-31   # The version number to use when ordering resources from an API or a Marketplace Item.(e..g., Microsoft.Compute/virtualmachines)     Required if you are using custom providers in your setup, otherwise not required
- type: "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments"  // (required). The resource provider namespace and name for the role assignment(e... For example - 'Microsoft.Authorization'). Also include ARM template link to reference this section as well   Required if you are using custom providers in your setup, otherwise not required
- properties:    # These contain specifics of a single or multiple resources that need being created/modified by the deployment(e... For example - 'name', ‘type’). Not all items needed here. The full list and details can be found at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates
